title: Parameters Translating to XML Incorrectly
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

I am trying to turn a Verilog module into xml output, and I am running into problems when encountering parameters. In the XML output below, it looks like verilator is unable to use the parameter and instead is providing constants from I'm not sure where. I am expecting a varref to the parameter inside of an sel tag.

My verilog/verilator experience is rather limited so please let me know if I am in the wrong here.

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

```verilog
module test_module();
	parameter [31:0]param = 'h1234abcd;
	reg count = param[1];
	reg indexed =param[2];						
endmodule
```
verilator --xml-only file_name -y dir

```xml
  <netlist>
    <module loc="c,1,8,1,19" name="test_module" origName="test_module">
      <var loc="c,2,17,2,22" name="param" dtype_id="1" vartype="logic" origName="param" param="true">
        <const loc="c,2,25,2,35" name="32'h1234abcd" dtype_id="1"/>
      </var>
      <var loc="c,3,5,3,10" name="count" dtype_id="2" vartype="logic" origName="count"/>
      <initialstatic loc="c,3,18,3,19">
        <assign loc="c,3,18,3,19" dtype_id="2">
          <const loc="c,3,18,3,19" name="1'h0" dtype_id="2"/>
          <varref loc="c,3,18,3,19" name="count" dtype_id="2"/>
        </assign>
      </initialstatic>
      <var loc="c,4,5,4,12" name="indexed" dtype_id="2" vartype="logic" origName="indexed"/>
      <initialstatic loc="c,4,19,4,20">
        <assign loc="c,4,19,4,20" dtype_id="2">
          <const loc="c,4,19,4,20" name="1'h1" dtype_id="2"/>
          <varref loc="c,4,19,4,20" name="indexed" dtype_id="2"/>
        </assign>
      </initialstatic>
    </module>
    <typetable loc="a,0,0,0,0">
      <basicdtype loc="c,3,1,3,4" id="2" name="logic"/>
      <basicdtype loc="c,2,25,2,35" id="1" name="logic" left="31" right="0"/>
    </typetable>
  </netlist>

```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

- Verilator 5.002 2022-10-29

What OS and distribution are you using?

- Ubuntu 20.04.5 LTS

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

- Please!
